Buffer Replacement — AI-ready guide

Goal
Build a cloud-first Buffer-style scheduler for X, Instagram, YouTube/Shorts, TikTok, Bluesky, and Threads with a modern accessible UI, scheduling, and media validation.

Key files (start here)
- README.md — project overview and dummy-proof getting started.
- ARCHITECTURE.md — components, data flow, platform notes.
- ACCESSIBILITY.md — WCAG 2.2 AA targets and how to check them.
- SETUP.md — environment expectations and upcoming secrets.
- USAGE.md — how the UI is meant to work once wired.
- TROUBLESHOOTING.md — quick fixes for common issues.
- PLAN.md — tasks with emoji status and history of requests.
- CHANGELOG.md — dated record of changes.
- COSTS.md — API/hosting estimates, limits, and savings tips.
- web/index.html — current static preview; CSS/JS live alongside.

Common tasks
- Open the UI preview locally: `cd buffer-replacement && start web/index.html` on Windows or `open web/index.html` on macOS.
- Run a quick accessibility scan on the static page: `cd buffer-replacement && npx pa11y web/index.html` (installs on demand).
- Update docs together when functionality changes; reflect user-facing impacts in README, USAGE, and PLAN.

Architecture highlights
- Frontend: static preview today; planned React/Next or similar SPA with accessible components and drag-and-drop scheduling.
- Backend (planned): API layer handling auth, scheduling, media upload/validation, and retries. Worker queue for publish attempts.
- Storage (planned): Postgres for data, Redis/queue for jobs, S3-compatible storage for cached media.
- Security: secrets in environment variables; no keys in git. `.env.example` will list names once defined.

Accessibility baseline
- Target WCAG 2.2 AA; semantic HTML, labelled inputs, focus styles, keyboard support.
- Automated spot-check: `cd buffer-replacement && npx pa11y web/index.html`.

Notes for LLM/agent use
- Keep responses concise and in plain UK English.
- Cite files/commands where required and keep updates small and reviewable.
